title: Cosmic Metabolism
definition: The eternal cycle of creation, dissolution, and rebirth in the universe, where matter, energy, and information are constantly recycled, reassembled, and relaunched into existence.

difficulty_levels.beginner: Imagine a forest. Mycelium is the underground network that supports all plant life. The universe can be seen as a vast mycelial network where everything - stars, planets, humans - are part of an endless cycle of creation, death, and rebirth.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Cosmic Metabolism refers to the idea that the universe operates not as a static collection of objects, but as a dynamic process where matter, energy, and information are constantly cycled. This view challenges traditional notions of creation and death by suggesting that everything is interconnected and part of a continuous cycle.
difficulty_levels.expert: In this concept, the universe is viewed as an ecosystem where every component undergoes a constant process of transformation. The fundamental particles that make up matter, energy, and information are never truly destroyed; instead, they are recycled and recombined into new forms. This cyclical view challenges our understanding of time, space, and the nature of reality itself.

extra_fields.insight: The universe is not a static collection of objects but a dynamic, cyclical process.
